title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chatbots And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's direction toward merging chatbots and agents into persistent personal AI systems. He argues that compute will be the decisive factor in AI competition, predicts interface metaphors will dissolve in favor of autonomous agents, and expects current premium AI capabilities to become commoditized.
writeup: In this interview, OpenAI president and co-founder Greg Brockman lays out a direction for merging chatbots and agents into persistent personal AI systems. Brockman argues that compute will be the decisive factor in AI competition, predicts that interface metaphors will dissolve in favor of autonomous agents, and expects today's premium AI capabilities to become commoditized. The discussion touches on test-time compute, personal AGI, and voice assistants as part of that trajectory.
